question: The t-V model of spinless fermions on a two-dimensional Lieb lattice is defined by nearest-neighbor hopping t and repulsive interaction V on a three-sublattice lattice (sublattices A, B, C per unit cell) at half-filling. Below a critical temperature T_c, the system spontaneously breaks particle-hole symmetry and develops a charge density wave. Quantum Monte Carlo simulations (auxiliary-field QMC) with finite-size scaling are performed for system sizes L=4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 15 and interaction strengths V/t = 0.75, 1, 1.5, 2, 3. What is the ORDER and UNIVERSALITY CLASS of the charge-density-wave phase transition of the t-V model on the Lieb lattice? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt", and "universality_class" using one of: "2D Ising", "3D Ising", "BKT", "mean-field", "other".

metadata.system: t-V model (spinless fermions with NN hopping t and repulsion V) on 2D Lieb lattice at half-filling
metadata.source_excerpt: A finite-size scaling analysis of the squared order parameter m^2... shows excellent data collapse when the critical exponents are fixed to the two-dimensional Ising universality class, confirming a continuous transition in the 2D Ising class
metadata.difficulty_note: CT-INT QMC has heavy-tailed distributions making variance ill-defined; AF-QMC is more reliable for this system. Weak-coupling Tc ~ V^2 (vs mean-field V), strong-coupling approaches classical Ising limit.
